name: Distributed Systems
description: Graduate-level survey of the principles behind large-scale distributed systems. Covers consistency models replication consensus fault tolerance and partitioning. Reading-heavy with hands-on Go labs. Auditors welcome.

name: Advanced Go Programming
description: Deep dive into idiomatic Go for backend engineers. Concurrency patterns the memory model context error handling generics profiling and building production services. Project-based with weekly code reviews.

name: Cloud Architecture and Infrastructure
description: Designing reliable scalable cloud systems on AWS. Covers compute storage networking IaC with Terraform Kubernetes observability and cost optimization. Hands-on labs on EC2 Lambda S3 and EKS.

name: Database Systems Internals
description: How relational and distributed databases actually work. Storage engines indexing query execution transactions isolation levels concurrency control and replication. Focus on PostgreSQL internals with reading from DDIA.

name: Kubernetes in Production
description: Running real workloads on Kubernetes. Deployments services ingress autoscaling RBAC operators and GitOps with ArgoCD. Hands-on labs on EKS with Terraform-provisioned clusters and homelab examples.

name: Site Reliability Engineering
description: Operating reliable systems at scale. SLIs SLOs error budgets incident response on-call practices observability and toil reduction. Based on the Google SRE books with practical alerting labs.

name: Concurrency in Go
description: Goroutines channels select the sync package context cancellation and common concurrency patterns and pitfalls. Detecting and fixing data races with the race detector. Workshop format with live coding.

name: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
description: Chapter-by-chapter reading group through Kleppmann's Designing Data-Intensive Applications. Storage retrieval encoding replication partitioning transactions and consistency. Weekly discussion and short writeups.

name: Terraform and Infrastructure as Code
description: Provisioning cloud infrastructure declaratively. HCL modules state management remote backends workspaces and CI pipelines. Building reusable AWS modules and managing drift. Labs target a sandbox AWS account.

name: Networking for Backend Engineers
description: TCP/IP HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 TLS load balancing DNS and debugging with tcpdump and Wireshark. Focused on what backend engineers actually hit in production rather than exam trivia.

name: System Design Interview Prep
description: Mock system design sessions for backend and infra roles. Estimation API design data modeling caching sharding and trade-off discussion. Peer-led with rotating interviewer practice.

name: PostgreSQL Performance Tuning
description: Reading EXPLAIN ANALYZE indexing strategies query planning vacuum and bloat connection pooling and partitioning. Hands-on tuning against a seeded workload. Complements the Database Systems course.

name: gRPC and Protocol Buffers
description: Building service-to-service APIs with gRPC and protobuf. Schema design streaming interceptors deadlines and backward compatibility. Implemented in Go with a multi-service example.

name: Observability with Prometheus and Grafana
description: Metrics logs and traces in practice. Instrumenting Go services with Prometheus building Grafana dashboards alerting rules and OpenTelemetry tracing. Labs run against the homelab cluster.

name: Security for Backend Developers
description: Practical application security. AuthN and AuthZ OAuth and JWT secrets management common web vulnerabilities and secure handling of data in transit and at rest. Threat modeling a sample Go service.

name: Functional Programming Concepts
description: Immutability pure functions higher-order functions algebraic data types and error handling without exceptions. Examples in multiple languages with takeaways applicable to idiomatic Go.

name: Event-Driven Architecture with Kafka
description: Designing systems around event streams. Kafka topics partitions consumer groups exactly-once semantics schema registry and the outbox pattern. Hands-on producers and consumers in Go.

name: Go Microservices Workshop
description: Building and deploying a small set of Go microservices end to end. Project layout configuration health checks graceful shutdown containerization and deployment to Kubernetes. Capstone project.

name: Linux Systems Programming
description: Processes threads signals file descriptors memory mapping and syscalls. How the runtime and the kernel interact. Useful background for debugging Go services under load. C and Go examples.

name: Technical Writing for Engineers
description: Writing clear design docs runbooks RFCs and blog posts. Structuring arguments writing for the reader and editing ruthlessly. Workshop with peer review of real engineering writing.
